result:
Implemented first-class note tags across create, edit, list, and search.

In [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go#L20), notes now carry parsed tag metadata, stored on disk as a `Tags:` line under the title. `create` and `edit` accept `--tag`, `--tags`, and `--clear-tags`; `edit` still opens `$EDITOR` when no inline body or tag changes are given. `list` and `search` now accept repeated tag filters and print tags as a fourth output column. `search` also supports tag-only queries, so `search --tag work` is valid.

I added coverage for tag creation, tag-only edits, clearing tags, and tag-filtered list/search behavior in [main_test.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main_test.go#L13), and documented the new CLI usage plus the note file format in [README.md](/home/changkun/null-codex/README.md#L7).

I could not run `gofmt` or `go test` here because this environment does not have `gofmt` or `go` on `PATH`.
